purpose: Enrich top 10 Knoxville permit-verified builder signals with lawful public business contact info and buyer buy-box capture scripts.
rules: Public permit activity is a demand signal, not a validated buyer buy box.
Promote only after geography, lot size, max price, close speed, package recipient, and kill criteria are captured.

rows.name: BALL HOMES
rows.sourceEvidence: Official contact page lists Knoxville Area Office phone 865.862.4774 and public customer service email.
rows.demandSignal: Appears on 74 Knoxville/Knox public building permit rows. Latest sample 25-B1284 at 8828 KEENBERG LN issued 2025-04-16.
rows.nextAction: Call public business contact and capture exact buy box; do not promote to validated buyer until price/geography/lot/kill criteria are captured.

buyBoxCapture.geography: ASK: exact Knoxville / Knox County zip codes, subdivisions, school zones, and no-go areas.
buyBoxCapture.lotSize: ASK: minimum/maximum lot size, frontage, infill vs subdivision lot preference.
buyBoxCapture.maxPrice: ASK: max raw lot price and price as % of expected finished-home sale.
buyBoxCapture.utilitiesAccess: ASK: sewer/septic, water, electric, road frontage and driveway constraints.
buyBoxCapture.killCriteria: ASK: slope, flood, wetlands, zoning, HOA, title, access, utility extension, soil/septic blockers.
buyBoxCapture.closeSpeed: ASK: decision-maker, due-diligence period, earnest money norm, and close timeline.
buyBoxCapture.packageFields: ASK: APN/parcel ID, map, dimensions, zoning, utilities, asking price, comps, seller terms, photos.

rows.callScript:
Hi, this is Okeito. I’m calling Knoxville Area Office / customer service at BALL HOMES. I track permit-verified land demand around Knoxville and noticed BALL HOMES has 74 recent public KGIS residential build signals, including 25-B1284 near 8828 KEENBERG LN.
I’m not calling with a random property. I’m trying to understand your exact land buy box so I only send lots that fit.
Quick questions:
1. Which Knoxville / Knox County zip codes or subdivisions are you actively buying lots in right now?
2. What lot size range works — infill, subdivision paper lots, quarter-acre, half-acre, acreage?
3. What is your max lot price or price-to-finished-home ratio before the deal is dead?
4. What kills a lot instantly: slope, flood, septic, sewer distance, no road frontage, HOA, title, utility extension, grade?
5. How fast can you close if title and due diligence are clean?
6. Who should receive lot packages — name, direct email, phone, and required fields?
If I find something matching that, do you want first look before I speak with other builders?

emailDraft.subject: Knoxville lots that fit BALL HOMES?
emailDraft.body:
Hi Knoxville Area Office / customer service,

I’m tracking public KGIS permit-backed builder activity in Knoxville. BALL HOMES shows 74 recent residential build signals, including 25-B1284 near 8828 KEENBERG LN.

I’m building a small list of off-market lots and only want to send properties that fit your actual buy box. What zip codes/subdivisions, lot sizes, max lot price, utility/access requirements, and deal killers should I screen for before sending anything?

If there is a better land/acquisitions contact, who should I send parcel packages to?

Thanks,
Okeito

Hi Contact not verified,

I’m tracking public KGIS permit-backed builder activity in Knoxville. DEFINITY CONSTRUCTION LLC shows 13 recent residential build signals, including IRC-NEW-25-0170 near 1908 AMHERST RD.

I’m building a small list of off-market lots and only want to send properties that fit your actual buy box. What zip codes/subdivisions, lot sizes, max lot price, utility/access requirements, and deal killers should I screen for before sending anything?

If there is a better land/acquisitions contact, who should I send parcel packages to?

Thanks,
Okeito
